programming.en: the process of writing instructions for a computer to follow

ex.en: She studies programming at university.

programming.en: the shows or programs broadcast by a TV or radio station

ex.en: The channel changed its evening programming.

prohibit.en: officially forbid something by law or rule

ex.en: Smoking is prohibited in this building.

project.en: a planned piece of work with a particular goal

ex.en: They are working on a new project.

project.en: estimate or forecast something based on current information

ex.en: Sales are projected to rise by ten percent.

project.en: show an image on a screen using light

ex.en: The teacher projected the slides onto the wall.

project.en: make your voice loud enough to be heard clearly at a distance

ex.en: The actor learned to project his voice on stage.
ex.ko: 그 배우는 무대에서 목소리를 크게 내는 법을 배웠다.

project.en: stick out or extend beyond a surface

ex.en: A balcony projects from the second floor.

projection.en: a prediction of future amounts or events based on current information

ex.en: The company's profit projections look promising.
ex.ko: 그 회사의 수익 전망은 밝아 보인다.

projection.en: an image shown on a screen using light from a projector

ex.en: The projection on the screen was blurry.

projection.en: a way of unconsciously blaming your own feelings or faults on someone else

ex.en: His anger was really a projection of his own fears.
ex.ko: 그의 분노는 사실 자신의 두려움이 투사된 것이었다.

prom.en: a formal dance held by a high school class, usually near the end of the year

ex.en: She bought a new dress for the prom.
ex.ko: 그녀는 프롬을 위해 새 드레스를 샀다.

prominence.en: the quality of being well known or important

ex.en: The scientist rose to prominence after her discovery.
ex.ko: 그 과학자는 그 발견 이후 유명해졌다.

prominence.en: the state of being given special importance or attention

ex.en: Climate change has gained prominence in political debates.
ex.ko: 기후 변화는 정치 토론에서 비중이 커졌다.

ex.en: The company gave a prompt response to the complaint.

prompt.en: cause someone to do something or make something happen

ex.en: The scandal prompted calls for his resignation.
ex.ko: 그 스캔들은 그의 사퇴 요구를 촉발했다.

prompt.en: a symbol or message on a screen showing that a computer or program is ready for input

ex.en: Type your password at the prompt.
She replied to the email promptly.

proponent.en: a person who supports or argues in favor of something

ex.en: She is a leading proponent of renewable energy.

proposed.en: suggested as a plan or idea, not yet agreed or decided

ex.en: The proposed changes will be discussed at the meeting.
ex.ko: 제안된 변경 사항은 회의에서 논의될 것이다.

prose.en: written or spoken language in its ordinary form, not poetry

ex.en: The novel is written in simple prose.
ex.ko: 그 소설은 평범한 산문으로 쓰여 있다.

prosecute.en: bring a criminal charge against someone in a court of law

ex.en: He was prosecuted for tax fraud.

prospect.en: the possibility that something good will happen in the future

ex.en: The job offers good career prospects.
ex.ko: 그 일자리는 전망 있는 커리어를 제공한다.

prospect.en: someone who might become a customer, employee, or successful person

ex.en: The sales team identified several new prospects.
ex.ko: 영업팀은 새로운 잠재 고객 몇 명을 발굴했다.

prospect.en: search an area for valuable minerals such as gold or oil

ex.en: They prospected for gold in the river.
The business prospered after the merger.

proudly.en: in a way that shows pride

ex.en: She proudly showed off her new diploma.

proven.en: shown to be true or effective

ex.en: This is a proven method for losing weight.

province.en: one of the large administrative regions that a country is divided into

ex.en: She grew up in a rural province.

province.en: the area of responsibility or expertise that belongs to someone

ex.en: Deciding the budget is not my province.
ex.ko: 예산을 정하는 것은 내 소관이 아니다.

provision.en: a clause in a law, contract, or agreement that states a condition

ex.en: The contract includes a provision for early termination.
ex.ko: 그 계약에는 조기 종료에 관한 조항이 있다.

provision.en: the act of supplying or providing something needed

ex.en: The charity focuses on the provision of clean water.
ex.ko: 그 자선단체는 깨끗한 물 공급에 주력한다.

provision.en: a supply of food and other necessary items

ex.en: They packed enough provisions for a week.

proximity.en: the state of being near or close to something

ex.en: The hotel's proximity to the beach was a big advantage.
ex.ko: 그 호텔이 해변에 가깝다는 점이 큰 장점이었다.

psychologically.en: in a way that relates to the mind or mental processes

ex.en: The experience was psychologically damaging.

psychology.en: the scientific study of the human mind and behavior

ex.en: She is majoring in psychology.

publication.en: a book, magazine, or other printed work made available to the public

ex.en: This magazine is a monthly publication.

publication.en: the act of producing and releasing a book, article, or other written work

ex.en: The publication of the report was delayed.

publicity.en: attention or information given to something to make the public aware of it

ex.en: The movie got a lot of publicity before its release.
ex.ko: 그 영화는 개봉 전 큰 홍보 효과를 얻었다.

publicize.en: make something known to the public

ex.en: The band publicized their new album on social media.
ex.ko: 그 밴드는 소셜 미디어에서 새 앨범을 홍보했다.

pudding.en: a soft, sweet dessert, often made with milk, eggs, or flour

ex.en: We had chocolate pudding for dessert.

pudding.en: (British) the dessert course of a meal, whatever it is

ex.en: What's for pudding tonight?

pulse.en: the regular beating of blood through your body, felt at the wrist or neck

ex.en: The nurse checked his pulse.

pulse.en: beat or throb with a strong, regular rhythm

ex.en: Music pulsed through the club.

pulse.en: a brief burst of electrical current, sound, or light

ex.en: The device sends out a pulse every second.

pulse.en: the edible seeds of plants such as peas, beans, and lentils

ex.en: Pulses are a great source of protein.

pumpkin.en: a large round orange vegetable often used to make pies or Halloween decorations

ex.en: They carved a pumpkin for Halloween.

punch.en: hit someone or something hard with your fist

ex.en: He punched the wall in anger.
ex.ko: 그는 화가 나서 벽을 주먹으로 쳤다.

punch.en: a hard hit with the fist

ex.en: She landed a punch on his jaw.

punch.en: make a hole in something using a tool

ex.en: The conductor punched our tickets.

punch.en: a sweet drink made by mixing fruit juices, sometimes with alcohol

ex.en: They served fruit punch at the party.

punctual.en: arriving or doing something at the exact time expected

ex.en: He is always punctual for meetings.
ex.ko: 그는 항상 회의 시간을 정확히 지킨다.

punctuality.en: the quality of doing things or arriving at the correct time

ex.en: Punctuality is highly valued in this company.
ex.ko: 이 회사에서는 시간 엄수를 매우 중요하게 여긴다.

ex.en: She purchased a new laptop online.

purchase.en: something bought, or the act of buying it

ex.en: This was my best purchase this year.
ex.ko: 이것은 올해 내가 한 최고의 구매였다.

purposeful.en: having a clear aim or determination

ex.en: She walked with a purposeful stride toward the office.
ex.ko: 그녀는 목적이 있는 발걸음으로 사무실을 향해 걸었다.

pursuit.en: the act of trying to achieve or obtain something over time

ex.en: He gave up his job in pursuit of his dream.
ex.ko: 그는 꿈을 추구하기 위해 직장을 그만두었다.

pursuit.en: the act of chasing someone or something to catch it

ex.en: The police began a high-speed pursuit of the car.
ex.ko: 경찰은 그 차를 고속으로 추적하기 시작했다.

pursuit.en: an activity that someone spends time on for interest or enjoyment

ex.en: Painting is one of her favorite leisure pursuits.
ex.ko: 그림 그리기는 그녀가 가장 좋아하는 여가 활동 중 하나이다.

puzzled.en: confused because you cannot understand something

ex.en: She gave me a puzzled look when I explained the plan.
ex.ko: 내가 계획을 설명하자 그녀는 어리둥절한 표정을 지었다.

qualification.en: a skill, degree, or official certificate that shows you are able to do something

ex.en: She has the qualifications to be a doctor.
ex.ko: 그녀는 의사가 될 자격을 갖추고 있다.

qualification.en: a statement that limits or adds a condition to something said before

ex.en: I'll agree, but with one qualification.
ex.ko: 동의는 하겠지만 한 가지 단서가 있다.

quantify.en: express or measure something as a number or amount

ex.en: It's hard to quantify how much stress affects performance.
ex.ko: 스트레스가 성과에 얼마나 영향을 미치는지 수량화하기는 어렵다.

quarantine.en: the state of being kept apart from others to stop a disease from spreading

ex.en: The travelers had to stay in quarantine for two weeks.
ex.ko: 그 여행객들은 2주 동안 격리 상태로 지내야 했다.

quarantine.en: keep a person or animal apart from others to prevent a disease from spreading

ex.en: The sick animals were quarantined from the rest of the herd.
The two brothers quarreled over money.
ex.ko: 그 두 형제는 돈 문제로 다투었다.

ex.en: They had a quarrel about the trip.

quarrelsome.en: often arguing with people over small things

ex.en: He becomes quarrelsome after a few drinks.
ex.ko: 그는 술을 좀 마시면 다투기 좋아하는 성격이 된다.

queer.en: strange or different from what is usual or expected

ex.en: There was something queer about the way he smiled.
ex.ko: 그가 웃는 방식에는 뭔가 기묘한 데가 있었다.

queer.en: relating to people whose sexual orientation or gender identity is not heterosexual or traditionally defined

ex.en: The festival celebrates queer film and art.
ex.ko: 그 축제는 퀴어 영화와 예술을 기념한다.

query.en: ask a question about something, often to check if it is correct

ex.en: A customer queried the extra charge on the bill.
ex.ko: 한 고객이 청구서의 추가 요금에 대해 문의했다.

query.en: a question, especially one asking for information or clarification

ex.en: Please send your query to customer service.

quotable.en: worth repeating or quoting because it is well said or memorable

ex.en: The speech was full of quotable lines.
ex.ko: 그 연설은 인용할 만한 문구로 가득했다.

quotation.en: a phrase or sentence taken exactly from a book, speech, or person

ex.en: She opened her essay with a famous quotation.

quotation.en: a statement of how much a job or service will cost

ex.en: We got a quotation from three different builders.
ex.ko: 우리는 세 곳의 건설업체로부터 견적을 받았다.

quote.en: repeat exactly what someone else said or wrote

ex.en: He quoted a line from his favorite movie.
ex.ko: 그는 자신이 가장 좋아하는 영화의 한 대사를 인용했다.

quote.en: a phrase or sentence repeated exactly from a book, speech, or person

ex.en: Can you find a good quote for the poster?
ex.ko: 포스터에 넣을 좋은 인용문을 찾아줄 수 있어?

quote.en: state the price you would charge for a job or service

ex.en: The mechanic quoted a price of $300 for the repair.

racism.en: the belief that one race is better than another, or unfair treatment based on race

ex.en: The company was criticized for racism in its hiring practices.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 채용 관행에서의 인종차별로 비판받았다.

racist.en: a person who believes one race is superior to another or treats people unfairly because of race

ex.en: He was fired for making racist comments.

racist.en: showing unfair prejudice or discrimination based on race

ex.en: The ad was criticized as racist.

rack.en: a frame or structure used for holding or storing things

ex.en: She hung her coat on the rack.

rack.en: think very hard, especially when trying to remember or solve something

ex.en: I racked my brain trying to remember his name.
ex.ko: 나는 그의 이름을 기억해내려고 머리를 쥐어짰다.

radar.en: a device that uses radio waves to detect and locate distant objects, such as planes or ships

ex.en: The plane appeared on the radar screen.

radiate.en: send out light, heat, or energy in all directions

ex.en: The stove radiated warmth throughout the room.
ex.ko: 그 난로는 방 전체에 온기를 발산했다.

radiate.en: clearly show a strong feeling or quality

ex.en: She radiated confidence during the interview.

radical.en: involving major, thorough change from what is usual or traditional

ex.en: The company made radical changes to its business model.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 사업 모델에 급진적인 변화를 주었다.

radical.en: a person who holds extreme political or social views and wants major change

ex.en: The group was led by young political radicals.
ex.ko: 그 단체는 젊은 정치 급진주의자들이 이끌었다.

radically.en: in a way that is thorough and complete; totally

ex.en: Her views changed radically after the trip.
ex.ko: 그녀의 생각은 그 여행 이후 근본적으로 바뀌었다.

radium.en: a rare radioactive metal element found in small amounts in certain ores

ex.en: Marie Curie discovered radium in 1898.

rafter.en: one of the long sloping wooden beams that support a roof

ex.en: Wooden rafters ran across the ceiling of the barn.
ex.ko: 헛간 천장에는 나무 서까래가 가로질러 있었다.

rag.en: a piece of old or torn cloth, often used for cleaning

ex.en: He wiped the table with a rag.

rainbow.cue: 비 온 뒤 하늘에 뜨는 색 띠
rainbow.en: an arc of colors that appears in the sky when the sun shines through rain

ex.en: A rainbow appeared after the storm.
We got caught in a sudden rainstorm.

rally.en: a large public meeting held to support a cause or show enthusiasm

ex.en: Thousands attended the political rally.
ex.ko: 수천 명이 그 정치 집회에 참석했다.

rally.en: come together again or recover strength after a difficulty

ex.en: The team rallied in the second half to win the game.
ex.ko: 그 팀은 후반전에 힘을 되찾아 경기에서 승리했다.

ex.en: The ram butted against the fence.

ram.en: the memory a computer uses to run programs while it is on

ex.en: This laptop has 16 gigabytes of RAM.
The truck rammed into the back of the car.
ex.ko: 그 트럭은 차의 뒷부분을 세게 들이받았다.

ramp.cue: 높이가 다른 두 곳을 잇는 경사면
ramp.en: a sloped surface that connects two different levels

ex.en: The store added a ramp for wheelchair access.
ex.ko: 그 매장은 휠체어 이용을 위해 경사로를 추가했다.

ranger.en: a person whose job is to look after and protect a forest or park

ex.en: A park ranger guided us through the trail.
ex.ko: 공원 관리원이 우리를 그 산책로로 안내했다.

rape.en: the crime of forcing someone to have sex against their will

ex.en: He was convicted of rape.

rape.en: force someone to have sex against their will

ex.en: The film depicts a woman who was raped.
ex.ko: 그 영화는 강간당한 한 여성을 그린다.

rash.en: acting or done too quickly, without thinking about the results

ex.en: Don't make any rash decisions while you're angry.
ex.ko: 화가 났을 때 경솔한 결정을 내리지 마라.

rash.en: an area of red spots on the skin, often caused by illness or allergy

ex.en: She broke out in a rash after eating the shrimp.
ex.ko: 그녀는 새우를 먹은 후 발진이 돋았다.

rattle.en: make a series of quick knocking sounds, or cause something to do so

ex.en: The old windows rattled in the wind.
The tough question rattled the candidate.
ex.ko: 그 어려운 질문은 그 후보자를 당황하게 만들었다.

ravage.en: cause severe and widespread damage to something

ex.en: The wildfire ravaged the forest.

ravage.en: severe damage caused by something destructive

ex.en: The ravages of the war were visible everywhere.

reaction.en: something you feel, say, or do because of something that has happened

ex.en: Her reaction to the news was pure joy.
ex.ko: 그 소식에 대한 그녀의 반응은 순전한 기쁨이었다.

reaction.en: (chemistry) a process in which substances change into different substances

ex.en: Mixing the two chemicals caused a violent reaction.
ex.ko: 그 두 화학물질을 섞자 격렬한 반응이 일어났다.

ex.en: The information is readily available online.
ex.ko: 그 정보는 온라인에서 손쉽게 구할 수 있다.

ex.en: He readily agreed to help.

real estate.en: property in the form of land and buildings

ex.en: She works in real estate.

realization.en: the moment when you understand or become aware of something clearly

ex.en: The realization hit her that she had made a mistake.

realization.en: the act of making something you hoped for actually happen

ex.en: The project is close to realization.

rear.en: located at the back of something

ex.en: Please use the rear entrance.
He sat in the rear of the bus.

rear.en: look after and educate a child until they become an adult

ex.en: She was reared by her grandparents.

reasonably.en: in a sensible and fair way

ex.en: He acted reasonably given the situation.
ex.ko: 그는 그 상황을 고려할 때 합리적으로 행동했다.

ex.en: The hotel room was reasonably clean.

rebel.en: a person who fights against the government or authority in power

ex.en: Rebel forces took control of the town.

rebel.en: resist or fight against authority, control, or a set of rules

ex.en: Teenagers often rebel against their parents' rules.

rebellious.en: resisting authority or control, or unwilling to obey rules

ex.en: She was a rebellious teenager.

recession.en: a period when a country's economy performs badly and business activity decreases

ex.en: Many people lost their jobs during the recession.
ex.ko: 많은 사람들이 경기 침체 동안 직장을 잃었다.

recipe.en: a set of instructions for preparing and cooking a dish

ex.en: She followed a recipe for chocolate cake.

recipe.en: a way of achieving a particular result

ex.en: Hard work is the recipe for success.

reckon.en: believe or think something, without being completely certain

ex.en: I reckon it will rain later.
The cost is reckoned at around two million dollars.

recognition.en: public acknowledgment or approval of someone's achievement

ex.en: She finally got recognition for her work.
ex.ko: 그녀는 마침내 자신의 일에 대해 인정받았다.

recognition.en: the act of identifying someone or something you have seen or known before

ex.en: There was no sign of recognition in his eyes.
ex.ko: 그의 눈에는 알아보는 기색이 전혀 없었다.

ex.en: She recollected her childhood in the countryside.

recollection.en: a memory of something from the past

ex.en: I have no recollection of that conversation.
ex.ko: 나는 그 대화에 대한 기억이 전혀 없다.

recommendable.en: good enough to be recommended to others

ex.en: This restaurant is highly recommendable.

recommendation.en: advice about what someone should do

ex.en: The doctor gave a recommendation to rest for a week.

recommendation.en: a written statement praising someone's skills or character, used to help them get a job or place

ex.en: Her professor wrote her a strong letter of recommendation.
ex.ko: 그녀의 교수는 강력한 추천서를 써 주었다.

reconsider.en: think again about a decision or opinion, and possibly change it

ex.en: The board agreed to reconsider the proposal.

recreation.en: activities done for enjoyment and relaxation in free time

ex.en: Swimming is her favorite form of recreation.
ex.ko: 수영은 그녀가 가장 좋아하는 오락 활동이다.

recruit.en: find and enroll new people to join an organization, company, or armed forces

ex.en: The company is recruiting new graduates.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 신입 졸업생을 채용하고 있다.

recruit.en: a person who has recently joined an organization or the military

ex.en: The new recruits began their training this week.

rectangular.en: having the shape of a rectangle, with four right angles

ex.en: The table has a rectangular top.
ex.ko: 그 탁자는 직사각형 상판을 가지고 있다.

redo.en: do something again, usually to fix or improve it

ex.en: I had to redo my homework because I lost it.

redundant.en: more than what is needed, or no longer necessary

ex.en: Some of the details in the report are redundant.
ex.ko: 그 보고서의 일부 세부 사항은 불필요하다.

redundant.en: (British) no longer needed for a job and therefore dismissed from work

ex.en: Two hundred workers were made redundant last year.

reed.cue: 강가에 자라는 속이 빈 긴 풀
reed.en: a tall grass-like plant with a hollow stem that grows near water

ex.en: Reeds grew along the edge of the river.

reference.en: a mention of something or someone

ex.en: She made no reference to the accident.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 사고에 대해 아무 언급도 하지 않았다.

reference.en: the act of looking at something for information

ex.en: Keep this manual for future reference.
ex.ko: 나중에 참고할 수 있도록 이 설명서를 보관하세요.

reference.en: a statement about someone's character and abilities, given by someone who knows them, usually for a job application

ex.en: Please provide two professional references.
ex.ko: 직장 관련 추천서 두 부를 제출해 주세요.

referent.en: (linguistics) the real thing or idea that a word or expression refers to

ex.en: In this sentence, 'he' has no clear referent.
ex.ko: 이 문장에서 'he'는 명확한 지시 대상이 없다.

refinery.en: a factory where a raw material such as oil or sugar is purified or processed

ex.en: The oil refinery employs thousands of workers.
ex.ko: 그 정유공장은 수천 명의 근로자를 고용하고 있다.
